2026-03-06.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, spiking oil-tanker rates.
2026-03-06.exit_case: none — blockade is 8 days old and fully ongoing with no ceasefire, negotiations, or reversal announced.
2026-03-06.assessment: Week 1. Iran blocked the Strait of Hormuz on Feb 28 after US-Israeli strikes. VLCC rates hit an all-time high of $423,736/day — an extraordinary spike. The blockade remains fully in force with zero signs of diplomatic resolution. BWET (Breakwave Tanker ETF) is described by CNBC as the 'under-owned way to play it,' indicating the position is still early and uncrowded. This is a classic acute chokepoint shock with a clean binary: the blockade either continues (rates stay extreme) or resolves (rates normalize). No resolution signal exists yet.
2026-03-06.exit_advice: Exit if/when Iran lifts the blockade or a credible ceasefire is signed — the acute catalyst resolves cleanly when Hormuz reopens. Watch for diplomatic back-channels, Iranian statements offering corridor negotiations, or tankers resuming unimpeded transit. Until then, rates stay elevated and the position holds.

Resume high conviction immediately if fresh coverage reappears next week.
2026-03-13.exit_advice: HOLD through one quiet week. The strait is still blocked and rates remain elevated. Exit only on concrete reversal (reopening announcement, ceasefire, diplomatic resolution). A single quiet week on a live chokepoint blockade is noise, not signal.

2026-04-03.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, spiking oil-tanker rates.
2026-04-03.exit_case: none — Hormuz blockade still in force; headline confirms it may persist post-war, not reverse.
2026-04-03.assessment: Trump's private signaling that he'd accept ending the war WITHOUT requiring Hormuz reopening is structurally bullish for the tanker thesis. It suggests the chokepoint closure outlasts the conflict itself — transforming what was a wartime disruption into potentially a persistent feature of a post-war settlement. VLCC rates at $423,736/day were already extreme; if the blockade persists even after hostilities end, the rate environment stays elevated far longer than a simple 'war ends → blockade lifts' scenario implied. Fresh headline resets silence decay. BWET remains clean and under-owned.
2026-04-03.exit_advice: HOLD — blockade persists and now appears durable beyond any potential ceasefire. The headline extends, not closes, the catalyst window. Only a concrete Hormuz reopening (tankers resuming normal transit) triggers exit — nothing in this news or prior weeks signals that.
